data.output:
=== ddx-18e55cd2 ===
ID:       ddx-18e55cd2
Title:    registry: global plugins dir resolution + project→global→baked-in plugin lookup precedence
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:config,  area:plugin,  area:cli,  kind:feature,  phase:3,  initiative:zero-footprint,  principle:skills-personas
Parent:   ddx-937de9fc
Deps:     ddx-937de9fc
Desc:     PROBLEM (foundation child of ddx-937de9fc)
Plugins resolve only to project-local paths today. internal/registry has no concept of a machine-global plugin tree (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>) nor a precedence order for runtime lookup. This child lands the path resolver and precedence that the --global install/update/init/doctor children all build on.

ROOT CAUSE
- No resolver maps (projectRoot, name) -> a concrete plugin dir with precedence.
- cli/internal/registry/registry.go exposes BuiltinRegistry/Find only; there is no GlobalPluginsDir / ResolvePluginDir.
- The XDG data-home helper xdgDataHome() in cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:89 is unexported; project root comes from ddxroot.Path() (ddxroot.go:75).
- cli/internal/registry/installer.go rejects any home-rooted (~) install target at installer.go:43, :79, :135 (FEAT-015). That guard must stay for project-scope installs; this child does NOT write any global tree, only resolves paths.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add registry.GlobalPluginsDir() string returning filepath.Join(<xdg-data-home>, 'ddx', 'global', 'plugins'), honoring XDG_DATA_HOME and the ~/.local/share fallback (mirror ddxroot.xdgDataHome semantics; export a shared helper rather than duplicating).
2. Add registry.ResolvePluginDir(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, name string) (dir, scope string, ok bool) with precedence:
=== ddx-aedb009d ===
ID:       ddx-aedb009d
Title:    install/update: add --global writing to XDG global plugins + agent-tier skill links
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:config,  area:plugin,  area:cli,  kind:feature,  phase:3,  initiative:zero-footprint,  principle:skills-personas
Parent:   ddx-937de9fc
Deps:     ddx-937de9fc, ddx-18e55cd2
Desc:     PROBLEM (child of ddx-937de9fc)
ddx install / ddx update only write project-local trees; there is no --global surface to install once per machine into the agent-tier skill paths every project reads.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go:38-40 registers only --force/--local; runInstall (install.go:43) always targets the project via registry.InstallPackage(pkg, f.WorkingDir).
- cli/cmd/update.go performUpdate (update.go:194) always targets workingDir.
- Agent-tier skill paths (~/.claude/skills, ~/.agents/skills) are never written by install/update.
- newPluginCommand (install.go:153) wraps install; plugin list/show must accept --global too.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Register a --global bool flag on newInstallCommand (install.go:23), the update command, and the plugin/plugins subcommand's list & show.
2. When --global is set, resolve the target via registry.GlobalPluginsDir()/<name> (from ddx-18e55cd2) and write the plugin content there through a sanctioned global-scope installer entrypoint (do NOT route through the project ~-rejecting path; add a global installer that permits the global root only).
=== ddx-7c3893ad ===
ID:       ddx-7c3893ad
Title:    install/update: project install honors in-tree vs convention mode via ddxroot.Path()
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:config,  area:plugin,  area:cli,  kind:feature,  phase:3,  initiative:zero-footprint,  principle:skills-personas
Parent:   ddx-937de9fc
Deps:     ddx-937de9fc, ddx-18e55cd2
Desc:     PROBLEM (child of ddx-937de9fc)
Non-global ddx install / ddx update assume an in-tree .ddx/. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the plugin must land under the XDG project dir (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/) with project-tier skill links pointing into it. Today install writes to the literal relative .ddx/plugins path.

ROOT CAUSE
- registry.InstallPackage(pkg, f.WorkingDir) and the default target defaultPackageRootTarget (installer.go:128) build a project-relative '.ddx/plugins/<name>' string; they do not route through ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) which already resolves in-tree vs convention (cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75).
- update.go performUpdate (update.go:194) and commitPluginChanges (install.go:210) likewise assume .ddx/ in-tree.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Resolve the project plugin root through ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name> for the non-global path so convention mode lands under the XDG project dir and in-tree mode stays under <project>/.ddx/plugins/.
2. Create project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name> pointing into whichever plugins root resolved.
3. ddx update (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.

=== ddx-c772fdf9 ===
ID:       ddx-c772fdf9
Title:    init: add --global umbrella install; plain init stops installing plugins (lazy-resolve)
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:config,  area:plugin,  area:cli,  kind:feature,  phase:3,  initiative:zero-footprint,  principle:skills-personas
Parent:   ddx-937de9fc
Deps:     ddx-937de9fc, ddx-18e55cd2, ddx-aedb009d
Desc:     PROBLEM (child of ddx-937de9fc)
ddx init bundles default-plugin install with manifest creation. There is no ddx init --global umbrella for first-time per-machine setup, and plain ddx init eagerly installs the ddx plugin into the project instead of letting it lazy-resolve from the global tree.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go runInit (init.go:73) -> initProject (init.go:120) calls registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir) at init.go:284, writing .ddx/plugins/ddx + .agents/skills/ddx + .claude/skills/ddx unconditionally.
- No --global flag on the init command.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add --global bool to the init command. ddx init --global installs the default ddx plugin into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (via the global installer from ddx-aedb009d), creates agent-tier links at ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx, and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.
2. Plain ddx init (no --global) creates <project>/.ddx/ but does NOT install plugins; plugins lazy-resolve on first use via registry.ResolvePluginDir (ddx-18e55cd2), preferring project over global. Remove/guard the eager InstallPackageFromFS call at init.go:284 for the no-flag path.

NON-SCOPE: install/update --global mechanics (ddx-aedb009d); doctor reporting.
=== ddx-ac6d4c22 ===
ID:       ddx-ac6d4c22
Title:    doctor: report Global Install and Project Install plugin layers
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:config,  area:plugin,  area:cli,  kind:feature,  phase:3,  initiative:zero-footprint,  principle:skills-personas
Parent:   ddx-937de9fc
Deps:     ddx-937de9fc, ddx-18e55cd2
Desc:     PROBLEM (child of ddx-937de9fc)
ddx doctor does not surface the two-layer plugin install topology. Operators cannot see whether the ddx plugin is installed globally, per-project, or only lazy-resolving.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/doctor.go checkInstalledPlugins (doctor.go:867) inspects only recorded project install state; it has no notion of the global tier or lazy resolution.

PROPOSED FIX
Add two diagnostic lines to ddx doctor, using registry.GlobalPluginsDir() and registry.ResolvePluginDir (ddx-18e55cd2):
- 'Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing'.
- 'Project Install (<resolved ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx>) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global'.

NON-SCOPE: install/update/init mechanics (sibling children).
=== ddx-406ae97b ===
ID:       ddx-406ae97b
Title:    docs: ADR skill-install-topology + CLAUDE.md/SKILL.md install precedence
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:config,  area:plugin,  area:cli,  kind:docs,  phase:3,  initiative:zero-footprint,  principle:skills-personas
Parent:   ddx-937de9fc
Deps:     ddx-937de9fc, ddx-18e55cd2, ddx-aedb009d, ddx-7c3893ad, ddx-c772fdf9, ddx-ac6d4c22
Desc:     PROBLEM (docs child of ddx-937de9fc)
The reintroduced two-layer install topology (global vs project, lazy resolution, agent-tier links) must be recorded in the specs. Note this deliberately REVERSES the 'ddx install --global retired / plugins project-local only' statement currently in CLAUDE.md (epic ddx-602ae31a zero-footprint, Principle 2) — the docs must reflect the new precedence.

ROOT CAUSE
- No ADR documents skill/plugin install topology.
- CLAUDE.md (repo root) Persona System section and the project-local-only note do not describe global vs project precedence.
- library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md 'How this skill works' and library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md (and the mirror under cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/skills/ddx/) do not document install locations / agent-tier paths.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add or update docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-025-skill-install-topology.md (or the next free ADR number) describing: global tier ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins, project tier ddxroot.Path()/plugins (in-tree vs convention), agent-tier links, runtime precedence project>global>baked-in, and that --global is reinstated.
2. Update repo-root CLAUDE.md: replace the 'plugins project-local only / --global retired' wording with the two-layer precedence and document it in the Persona System section.
3. Update library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md 'How this skill works' and reference/agents.md to document install locations and agent-tier paths.
=== ddx-937de9fc ===
ID:       ddx-937de9fc
Title:    skills/personas/plugins: install-to-agent vs install-in-project — and ddx plugin commands honor both
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:config, area:plugin, area:cli, kind:feature, phase:3, initiative:zero-footprint, principle:skills-personas
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-602ae31a
Deps:     ddx-06cbaa90, ddx-04566a8f, ddx-06f820f4, ddx-64ecd687, ddx-c10d5af2, ddx-2bb4e0a5, ddx-b55fd606, ddx-a7ca3f7b, ddx-4c58ea3e
Desc:     PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.

217:Plugin and persona lookup follows a three-layer precedence: project-local (`<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`) → global (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/`) → baked-in binary default (only for the `ddx` plugin). The project layer always wins when present. See `docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md` for the full decision record.
